meaning: adj.科西嘉的,科西嘉人的,科西嘉方言的
examples: Well, amiable Corsican, let us suppose it is providence.
We had forgotten to say that Jacopo was a Corsican.
"But," said Franz, "the Corsican bandits that were among the crew of his vessel?"
Yet only 60% of islanders can now hold a conversation in Corsican, against 80% in 1977.
One has that trademark Californian drawl, the other was always trying to cover up his native Corsican accent.
